laws.title: Texas Minimum Wage — Federal Rate ($7.25/hr)
laws.summary: Texas has no state minimum wage higher than the federal $7.25/hour. Texas is a right-to-work state. Local governments are preempted from setting higher wages or additional employment mandates (see Austin and San Antonio preemption lawsuits). Texas has limited state-specific employment law obligations beyond federal law.
compliance_actions: Texas has no state paid leave mandate — voluntary programs are encouraged for recruitment
Ensure required federal posters are displayed at Texas worksites
Texas Commission on Human Rights Act applies to employers with 15+ employees — mirrors Title VII

laws.title: Texas Non-Compete Agreements — Enforceability Requirements
laws.summary: Texas allows non-compete agreements if they are ancillary to an otherwise enforceable agreement, supported by consideration (typically access to trade secrets or confidential information), and reasonable in scope, geography, and duration. Courts may reform — but not void — overly broad agreements. The FTC non-compete rule that would have banned most non-competes was blocked by a Texas federal court.
compliance_actions: Ensure Texas non-competes are tied to a legitimate business interest (trade secrets, specialized training, etc.)
Include non-competes in offer letters or agreements where confidential information will be shared
Consult employment counsel on duration and scope — courts tend to reform rather than void overbroad agreements
Note: The blocked FTC rule does not change Texas state law on non-competes
